requirements.location:
nbqa/replace_source.py — function _print_diff;
      nbqa/text.py — module-level color constants
requirements.description:
The _print_diff function in replace_source.py does not apply ANSI color codes to the
      "---", "+++" or "@@" diff header lines, and applies color codes in the wrong position
      for "-" and "+" content lines (RESET appended at end instead of prepended to next line).
      text.py is also missing the BOLD_WHITE ("\x1b[1;37m") and CYAN ("\x1b[36m") constants
      required for diff header formatting.
      This causes all --nbqa-diff tests to produce output with incorrect ANSI escape sequences
      compared to what tests assert.
requirements.contract:
text.py must export two additional constants:
        BOLD_WHITE = "\x1b[1;37m"   (bold bright white; used for --- and +++ lines)
        CYAN       = "\x1b[36m"     (cyan; used for @@ hunk-header lines)

      _print_diff must format each diff line as follows:
        - "---" lines  : prepend BOLD_WHITE  (no RESET before the very first diff line)
        - "+++" lines  : prepend RESET + BOLD_WHITE
        - "@@" lines   : prepend RESET + CYAN
        - "-"  lines   : prepend RESET + RED   (no trailing RESET on the line itself)
        - "+"  lines   : prepend RESET + GREEN (no trailing RESET on the line itself)
        - context lines: prepend RESET

      The list appended after line_changes must be [RESET + "\n"] instead of ["\n"], so
      that the final color applied to the last content line is properly reset.

      The function signature and return value (bool) must remain unchanged.
      The "Cell N" header block and "------" separator lines must remain uncolored (as-is).

      Concrete expected format for a single-cell diff output:
        "\x1b[1mCell N\x1b[0m\n"
        "------\n"
        "\x1b[1;37m--- {filepath}\n"
        "\x1b[0m\x1b[1;37m+++ {filepath}\n"
        "\x1b[0m\x1b[36m@@ ... @@\n"
        "\x1b[0m\x1b[31m-{removed line}\n"
        "\x1b[0m\x1b[32m+{added line}\n"
        "\x1b[0m\n"

requirements.location:
nbqa/replace_source.py — functions diff and mutate;
      nbqa/replace_source.py — function _get_new_source (indirectly via diff)
requirements.description:
The diff() function calls cell["source"][-1] += "\n" and new_source[-1] += "\n" before
      computing the unified_diff. When a third-party tool removes all non-separator content from
      a cell (resulting in an empty cell body), _get_new_source returns an empty list [].
      Accessing index [-1] on an empty list raises IndexError, which is caught by the outer
      except Exception handler in _post_process_notebooks and stored as a failed notebook,
      suppressing the expected diff output entirely.
      Affected tests use a local "remove_all" tool that strips every non-"#" line from the
      temporary Python file, making cell bodies empty after the tool runs.
requirements.contract:
diff() must guard against empty source/new_source lists before accessing [-1].

      When cell["source"] is non-empty, append "\n" to its last element as today.
      When cell["source"] is empty, treat it as-is (no mutation needed).

      When new_source is non-empty, append "\n" to its last element as today.
      When new_source is empty, represent it as ["\n"] for the purpose of unified_diff
      (so that the diff shows all original lines as removed and an empty replacement).

      The unified_diff call and _print_diff behavior must remain unchanged.
      The function signature and return value (bool) must remain unchanged.

requirements.description:
The module nbqa.save_code_source does not exist. The current module is named
      nbqa.save_source. The test test_hash_collision uses monkeypatch.setattr on
      "nbqa.save_code_source.CODE_SEPARATOR" to simulate a hash collision scenario.
      Because the module name does not match, pytest raises ImportError at test collection:
        "import error in nbqa.save_code_source: No module named 'nbqa.save_code_source'"
      For monkeypatching to correctly affect the CODE_SEPARATOR used inside _parse_cell,
      that constant must live in nbqa.save_code_source (the module being patched), not in
      a separate module with a different name.
requirements.contract:
The file nbqa/save_source.py must be renamed to nbqa/save_code_source.py.
      All public symbols (CODE_SEPARATOR, MAGIC, NEWLINE, NEWLINES, TRANSFORMED_MAGICS,
      Index, main, and all private helpers) remain in this renamed module.

      Every import of nbqa.save_source or from nbqa import ... save_source throughout the
      nbqa package (currently in nbqa/replace_source.py and nbqa/__main__.py) must be
      updated to reference nbqa.save_code_source instead.

      The public API (function signatures, return types, exported constants) must be
      identical to the current nbqa/save_source.py.
requirements.acceptance_tests:
tests/test_hash_collision.py::test_hash_collision
      All previously passing tests that exercised save_source functionality must continue
      to pass (regression check).

requirements.description:
When a third-party tool removes all CODE_SEPARATOR markers from the temporary Python
      file (e.g. "remove_comments" strips all "#" characters, including the separator which
      starts with "# %%NBQA-CELL-SEP..."), calling next(pycells) exhausts the iterator
      prematurely and raises StopIteration.
      This StopIteration is currently caught by the generic "except Exception" handler in
      _post_process_notebooks and stored as repr(StopIteration()), which results in an
      error message containing "StopIteration()" rather than the expected user-facing message.
      The test asserts:
        "Tool did not preserve code separators and cannot be safely used with nbQA" in err
      but the current message does not contain this phrase.
requirements.contract:
In both mutate() and diff(), any StopIteration raised while iterating over pycells
      (i.e., from next(pycells)) must be caught and re-raised as a different exception type
      (e.g., ValueError) whose string representation contains the exact phrase:
        "Tool did not preserve code separators and cannot be safely used with nbQA"

      The re-raised exception will then be caught by the outer except Exception handler in
      _post_process_notebooks and stored in failed_notebooks. The existing
      _print_failed_notebook_errors path will then include this message in stderr, satisfying
      the test assertion.

      The function signatures and return types of mutate() and diff() must remain unchanged.

requirements.description:
The --nbqa-md CLI flag does not exist in nbqa's argument parser. When tests pass
      "--nbqa-md" to main(), it is forwarded unchanged to the third-party tool as an
      unrecognized argument, causing the tool to fail with "unrecognized arguments: --nbqa-md"
      and producing no notebook output.
      Multiple tests require this flag to trigger a mode where nbqa operates on the
      MARKDOWN cells of a notebook (cell_type == "markdown") rather than code cells.
      In this mode, markdown cell contents are extracted to a temporary .md file, the
      third-party tool is run against that .md file, and the results are written back into
      the notebook's markdown cells.
requirements.contract:
CLI (cmdline.py):
        Add --nbqa-md as a boolean/store_true flag to the argument parser.
        It must be consumed by nbqa and NOT forwarded to the third-party tool.
        CLIArgs must expose an md attribute reflecting this flag.

      Config (config.py):
        Add an "md" key (bool, default False) to the Configs TypedDict and get_default_config.

      save_md_source.py (new module):
        Provide a main() function with a signature compatible with save_source.main()
        (accepting notebook_json, file_descriptor, skip_celltags, etc.) that:
          - Iterates only over cells with cell_type == "markdown".
          - Writes the concatenated markdown cell content to the temporary file,
            separating cells with a distinct MD_CELL_SEPARATOR constant
            (similar to CODE_SEPARATOR in save_source).
          - Records a mapping from cell positions in the .md file back to notebook
            markdown cell indices (analogous to NotebookInfo).
          - Respects skip_celltags: markdown cells whose metadata tags include any
            tag in skip_celltags must be excluded from processing.
          - Returns an object (or NotebookInfo-compatible structure) containing the
            cell mapping and the set of markdown cell indices that were processed.

      replace_md_source.py (new module):
        Provide mutate() and diff() functions with signatures compatible with those in
        replace_source.py, operating on the temporary .md file and the notebook's
        markdown cells (using the mapping returned by save_md_source.main()).

      __main__.py:
        When configs["md"] is True, the pipeline must:
          1. Use a .md suffix (not .py) for the temporary file.
          2. Call save_md_source.main() instead of save_source.main().
          3. Pass the .md temporary file to the third-party tool.
          4. Call replace_md_source.diff() or replace_md_source.mutate() for post-processing.
        When configs["md"] is False, all existing behaviour is unchanged.

      Cell numbering for --nbqa-md diff output:
        Markdown cells must be numbered sequentially starting from 1 (counting only markdown
        cells, not code cells), matching the "Cell N" header format used for code-cell diffs.

      Error handling:
        Invalid notebooks that fail JSON parsing must be handled identically to the code-cell
        pipeline (recorded in failed_notebooks and reported via _print_failed_notebook_errors).
